calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-11-2022 Off Calendar

Motion To (1) Deem This Case Is Related To A Pending Unlawful Detainer Case, Case No. Cud-22-669241; (2) To
Consolidate The Two Cases; And (3) For A Temporary Restraining Order Staying Proceedings In The Ud Case Pending Determination On Consolidation Or, In The Alternative, Pending Resolution Of This Case; And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Aug-01-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF WIRELESS SOLUTIONS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED Motion To (1) Deem This Case Is Related To A Pending Unlawful Detainer Case, Case No. Cud-22-669241; (2) To
Consolidate The Two Cases; And (3) For A Temporary Restraining Order Staying Proceedings In The Ud Case Pending Determination On Consolidation Or, In The Alternative, Pending Resolution Of This Case is OFF CALENDAR, motion heard on July 29, 2022. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not reported (D501)

MOTION TO (1) DEEM THIS CASE IS RELATED TO A PENDING UNLAWFUL DETAINER CASE, CASE NO. CUD-22-669241; (2) TO
CONSOLIDATE THE TWO CASES; AND (3) FOR A TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER STAYING PROCEEDINGS IN THE UD CASE PENDING DETERMINATION ON CONSOLIDATION OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, PENDING RESOLUTION OF THIS CASEF
Off Calendar Jul-29-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: MOTION TO (1) DEEM THIS CASE IS RELATED TO A PENDING UNLAWFUL DETAINER CASE, CASE NO. CUD-22-669241; (2) TO
CONSOLIDATE THE TWO CASES; AND (3) FOR A TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER STAYING PROCEEDINGS IN THE UD CASE PENDING DETERMINATION ON CONSOLIDATION OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, PENDING RESOLUTION OF THIS CASE is DENIED. Unlawful detainer is a summary proceeding and the moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that the circumstances of this case give rise to any known exception warranting consolidation. The Court further notes that the issue of validity of the termination clause is raised as an affirmative defense in the unlawful detainer case providing a remedy at law regarding such clause. The propriety of the notice of termination is at the heart of unlawful detainer action for possession and is addressed therein. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

Motion To (1) Deem This Case Is Related To A Pending Unlawful Detainer Case, Case No. Cud-22-669241; (2) To
Consolidate The Two Cases; And (3) For A Temporary Restraining Order Staying Proceedings In The Ud Case Pending Determination On Consolidation Or, In The Alternative, Pending Resolution Of This Case; And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof

MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF WIRELESS SOLUTIONS, LLC's Motion To (1) Deem This Case Is Related To A Pending Unlawful Detainer Case, Case No. Cud-22-669241; (2) To Consolidate The Two Cases; And (3) For A Temporary Restraining Order Staying Proceedings In The UD Case Pending Determination On Consolidation Or, In The Alternative, Pending Resolution Of This Case.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Transferred to department 501 to be heard at 9:30 a.m. on August 1, 2022. That department handles all landlord/tenant actions per SF Local Rule 8.10A1.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
